custom.en: on one hand...on the other hand

custom.he: עד, לא יאוחר מ-, ליד, בסמוך ל-
מאמן, לאמן, להדריך, קרון נוסעים, כרכרה
מדויק. אמיתי, צודק, הוגן, רק, בלבד
עיר בירה, ראשי, משמעותי, הון, אות גדולה
נתון ל-, בכפוך ל-, למסור, להעביר לבעלות אחר, נתין, אזרח, אדם, נושא
כלל, חוק, שלטון, לשלוט, לפסוק, להחליט
קנס, קטן, עדין, דקיק, משביע רצון, משובח, מצוין
מעידה, פליטת פה, פרק זמן, פקיעה, סיום
מסלול לימודים, ראשי, עיקרי, סולם מז'ור (במוזיקה)
לפרסם; פרסום, לשלוח בדואר ; דואר, להציב, למנות, עמדה, משרה, תפקיד, עמוד

לנסוע בקביעות לעבודה / להמתיק עונש / להמיר, להחליף

customTexts.title: The Mystery of the Nazca Lines
customTexts.content:
Located in the arid coastal plains of Peru, the Nazca Lines are massive geoglyphs etched into the desert floor. Created between 500 BCE and 500 CE by the Nazca culture, these giant drawings depict various animals, plants, and geometric shapes. Because of their immense size, the figures can only be fully fully appreciated from an aerial view, leading to numerous theories about their purpose.

While some researchers speculate that the lines served as an astronomical calendar, others believe they had religious significance, possibly acting as pathways for ceremonial rituals related to water. Fortunately, the extremely dry and windless climate of the region has naturally preserved these extraordinary creations for centuries.

questions.q: 1. What is the primary subject of the text?
options: The daily lives of the ancient Nazca people.
The climate changes in the Peruvian desert.
The characteristics and theories surrounding the Nazca Lines.
The history of aerial photography in archaeology.
questions.explanation: שאלת רעיון מרכזי: הפסקה הראשונה מציגה את קווי נסקה (צורתם וגודלם), והפסקה השנייה דנה בתיאוריות לגבי מטרתם. תשובה C מסכמת זאת בצורה המדויקת ביותר.

questions.q: 2. The word 'arid' in the first paragraph is closest in meaning to:
options: dry
elevated
populated
mysterious
questions.explanation: הבנת מילה מהקשר: גם אם לא ידעת את משמעות המילה, בסוף הטקסט נכתב 'extremely dry and windless climate'. זהו רמז מקדים (Context Clue) לכך שהאזור הוא dry (יבש/צחיח).

questions.q: 3. Why have the Nazca Lines survived for so long?
options: They were protected by local governments.
The ancient culture used a special type of durable rock.
They are located in a region with very little rain or wind.
They are hidden deep underground.
questions.explanation: איתור פרטים: המשפט האחרון אומר מפורשות שהקווים השתמרו בזכות ה-'extremely dry and windless climate' (אקלים יבש ונטול רוחות).

questions.q: 4. What can be inferred about the creators of the Nazca Lines?
options: They possessed early flying machines.
They invested significant effort without being able to see the complete figures from the ground.
They were primarily an agricultural society.
They destroyed the lines after completing their rituals.
questions.explanation: שאלת הסקה (Inference): הטקסט מציין שהקווים עצומים ואפשר לראות אותם בשלמותם רק מהאוויר (aerial view). מכיוון שבשנת 500 לספירה לא היו כלי טיס, אפשר להסיק שהיוצרים השקיעו מאמץ רב ביצירת משהו שהם עצמם לא יכלו לראות בשלמותו מהקרקע.

questions.q: 5. Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a possible purpose for the lines?
options: A tool to predict the weather
An astronomical calendar
Pathways for religious ceremonies
Rituals connected to water
questions.explanation: שאלת אלימינציה (NOT): סורקים את תחילת הפסקה השנייה. מוזכרים שם לוח שנה אסטרונומי (B), טקסים דתיים (C), ופולחני מים (D). חיזוי מזג האוויר (A) לא מוזכר כלל.

customTexts.title: Artificial Intelligence in Medicine
customTexts.content:
Artificial Intelligence (AI) is rapidly revolutionizing the healthcare industry. By analyzing vast amounts of medical data at unprecedented speeds, AI systems can assist doctors in diagnosing diseases more accurately and earlier than ever before. For instance, machine learning algorithms are now highly proficient at detecting abnormalities in X-rays and MRI scans.

Furthermore, AI accelerates drug discovery by predicting how different chemical compounds will interact, saving years of laboratory research. However, this technological shift is not without concerns. Many experts emphasize that while AI is an indispensable tool, it lacks the human empathy and ethical judgment required in patient care, ensuring that doctors will not become obsolete anytime soon.

questions.q: 1. What is the main benefit of AI in analyzing medical images?
options: It completely replaces the need for radiologists.
It can identify abnormalities quickly and accurately.
It creates physical 3D models of the scans.
It reduces the radiation exposure for patients.
questions.explanation: איתור פרטים: בסוף הפסקה הראשונה מצוין שאלגוריתמים מיומנים ב-'detecting abnormalities in X-rays' (זיהוי חריגות). התשובה B משקפת זאת במדויק.

questions.q: 2. The word 'indispensable' in the second paragraph is closest in meaning to:
options: unnecessary
expensive
essential
dangerous
questions.explanation: אוצר מילים מהקשר: המשפט אומר 'while AI is an indispensable tool, it lacks human empathy' (בעוד שה-AI הוא כלי ___, חסרה לו אמפתיה). מילת הניגוד while רומזת שלמרות החיסרון, הכלי הוא הכרחי/חיוני (essential).

questions.q: 3. How does AI contribute to drug discovery?
options: By legally approving new medications.
By testing drugs on human volunteers.
By predicting chemical interactions rapidly.
By manufacturing the chemicals in factories.
questions.explanation: סריקה ממוקדת: חפש את הביטוי 'drug discovery' בפסקה השנייה. מיד אחריו נכתב: 'by predicting how different chemical compounds will interact'. תשובה C היא תרגום מדויק של משפט זה.

questions.q: 4. Based on the text, why won't doctors be replaced by AI?
options: Because AI systems are too expensive to maintain.
Because patients refuse to be treated by computers.
Because AI cannot perform surgical operations yet.
Because AI lacks empathy and ethical judgment.
questions.explanation: הסקה: המשפט האחרון מסביר שרופאים לא יהפכו למיותרים (obsolete) מכיוון של-AI חסר 'human empathy and ethical judgment' (אמפתיה אנושית ושיפוט מוסרי).

questions.q: 5. What is the author's tone regarding AI in medicine?
options: Completely pessimistic
Balanced and objective
Highly skeptical
Sarcastic
questions.explanation: שאלת נימה (Tone): המחבר מציג את היתרונות האדירים של AI (מהירות, אבחון, תרופות), אך גם מציג את המגבלות שלו (חוסר באמפתיה). הצגת שני הצדדים מעידה על נימה מאוזנת ואובייקטיבית (Balanced and objective).

customTexts.title: The Psychology of Colors in Marketing
customTexts.content:
In the world of marketing, colors are not chosen randomly; they are strategically selected to evoke specific emotional responses from consumers. Studies in color psychology demonstrate that warm colors like red and yellow can create a sense of urgency and excitement, which is why they are frequently used in clearance sales and fast-food logos.

Conversely, cool colors such as blue and green are employed to convey trust, security, and tranquility. Banks and tech companies heavily favor blue for this reason. However, marketers must also be aware of cultural differences, as the psychological impact of a color is not universally identical. For example, while white signifies purity in many Western cultures, it is associated with mourning in some Eastern traditions. Ultimately, color is a subtle yet powerful tool of persuasion.

questions.q: 1. Why do fast-food restaurants often use red and yellow in their logos?
options: Because these colors are cheaper to print.
Because they make the logos look more modern.
Because they create feelings of excitement and urgency.
Because they symbolize health and nature.
questions.explanation: איתור פרטים: הטקסט קושר מפורשות בין צבעים חמים (red, yellow) לתחושה של דחיפות והתרגשות (urgency and excitement), ולכן נעשה בהם שימוש ברשתות מזון מהיר.

questions.q: 2. The word 'convey' in the second paragraph is closest in meaning to:
options: hide
communicate
destroy
purchase
questions.explanation: אוצר מילים: המשפט אומר שצבעים קרים משמשים כדי 'convey trust' (לשדר/להעביר אמון). המילה המקבילה באנגלית היא communicate.

questions.q: 3. What is the main contrast presented between Western and Eastern cultures in the text?
options: Westerners prefer blue, while Easterners prefer red.
The color white represents purity in the West but mourning in the East.
Eastern cultures do not use color psychology in marketing.
Western cultures use colors to create urgency, unlike Eastern cultures.
questions.explanation: שאלת הבנת ניגוד: המילה 'while' (בעוד ש) מציגה את הניגוד: לבן מסמל טוהר במערב, אבל קשור לאבל (mourning) במזרח. תשובה B מתארת זאת במדויק.

questions.q: 4. According to the text, why do banks commonly use the color blue?
options: To create a sense of urgency.
To represent financial wealth.
To convey a feeling of trust and security.
To stand out from tech companies.
questions.explanation: סריקה מהירה: חפש את המילה Banks. מיד לפניה נכתב שצבעים קרים כמו כחול משדרים 'trust, security' (אמון, ביטחון).

questions.q: 5. What can be concluded about a global marketing campaign?
options: It should use the exact same colors everywhere to maintain brand identity.
It must adapt its color choices based on the target audience's cultural background.
It should avoid using white in any of its advertisements.
It relies solely on red and yellow to maximize sales.
questions.explanation: הסקה כללית: מכיוון שהטקסט מדגיש שיש הבדלים תרבותיים ('cultural differences') במשמעות הצבעים, מסיקים שקמפיין עולמי חייב להתאים את עצמו לתרבות המקומית כדי למנוע הבנה שגויה.

customTexts.title: Biomimicry: Innovation Inspired by Nature
customTexts.content:
For billions of years, nature has been perfecting its designs through evolution. Today, engineers and scientists are increasingly turning to the natural world for inspiration, a practice known as biomimicry. This approach involves studying nature's best ideas and imitating these designs to solve complex human problems.

A famous example of biomimicry is the invention of Velcro. In 1941, a Swiss engineer noticed how plant burrs clung tenaciously to his dog's fur. By examining the tiny hooks under a microscope, he replicated the mechanism to create a reusable fastener. Another remarkable instance is the Japanese Shinkansen bullet train. Its original design created a loud sonic boom when exiting tunnels. By modeling the front of the train after the streamlined beak of a kingfisher bird, engineers eliminated the noise while simultaneously increasing the train's speed and efficiency.

questions.q: 1. What is the best definition of 'biomimicry' according to the text?
options: The process of genetically modifying plants and animals.
The study of how human inventions negatively impact the environment.
Solving human problems by copying designs found in nature.
The evolutionary process that takes billions of years.
questions.explanation: רעיון מרכזי והגדרות: הטקסט מגדיר במפורש את המושג biomimicry כ-'studying nature's best ideas and imitating these designs to solve complex human problems'. תשובה C היא פרפרזה למשפט זה.

questions.q: 2. What inspired the invention of Velcro?
options: The streamlined beak of a bird.
Tiny hooks found on plant burrs.
A microscopic study of human hair.
The sonic boom of a train.
questions.explanation: איתור פרטים: הטקסט מציין שהמהנדס השוויצרי שם לב לאופן שבו קוצי צמחים (plant burrs) נדבקו לפרוות הכלב שלו, וזה מה שהוביל להמצאת הוולקרו.

questions.q: 3. The word 'efficiency' in the last sentence is closest in meaning to:
options: appearance
cost
effectiveness
danger
questions.explanation: אוצר מילים מהקשר: המשפט מדבר על שיפור שגרם לרכבת להיות מהירה ושקטה יותר, ולכן היא גם העלתה את ה-efficiency (יעילות/אפקטיביות).

questions.q: 4. What problem did the Japanese engineers face with the original bullet train?
options: It was too slow compared to airplanes.
It consumed too much fuel.
It produced a loud noise when leaving tunnels.
Its front beak broke frequently.
questions.explanation: איתור בעיה ופתרון: הטקסט כותב 'Its original design created a loud sonic boom when exiting tunnels'. כלומר, הבעיה הייתה הרעש העז (בום על-קולי) ביציאה ממנהרות.

questions.q: 5. What role did the kingfisher bird play in solving the train's problem?
options: Its feathers were used to insulate the train.
Its flight pattern helped map the train's route.
Its beak shape inspired the redesign of the train's front.
Its sound was used to mask the noise of the train.
questions.explanation: הבנת הקשרים: המהנדסים עיצבו את חזית הרכבת (modeling the front) על פי המקור האווירודינמי (beak) של הציפור (kingfisher) כדי לפתור את בעיית הרעש.

customTexts.title: The Growing Problem of E-Waste
customTexts.content:
As technology advances at a staggering pace, consumers constantly upgrade their smartphones, computers, and household appliances. Consequently, electronic waste, or e-waste, has become one of the fastest-growing waste streams in the world. When electronic devices are discarded improperly, they pose a severe threat to the environment. Devices often contain toxic substances such as lead, mercury, and cadmium, which can leak into the soil and groundwater if they end up in standard landfills.

While recycling e-waste is crucial, it is a complex process. Electronics are intricate assemblies of glass, plastic, and precious metals that are difficult to separate. Environmentalists advocate for a circular economy, where manufacturers design products that are easier to repair and recycle, rather than relying on planned obsolescence.

questions.q: 1. What is the primary cause of the increase in e-waste according to the text?
options: A lack of available recycling centers.
The frequent upgrading of electronic devices by consumers.
The decrease in the quality of modern technology.
The use of toxic materials in manufacturing.
questions.explanation: שאלת סיבה ותוצאה: המשפט הראשון אומר 'consumers constantly upgrade their smartphones...' ומיד אחריו המילה 'Consequently' (כתוצאה מכך) מקשרת את זה לגידול בפסולת אלקטרונית.

questions.q: 2. Why is improperly disposed e-waste considered dangerous?
options: It takes up too much space in landfills.
It is highly explosive when exposed to heat.
It contains toxic chemicals that can contaminate soil and water.
It emits harmful radiation into the atmosphere.
questions.explanation: איתור פרטים: הטקסט אומר שמכשירים מכילים חומרים רעילים (toxic) שיכולים לדלוף לקרקע ולמי התהום (soil and groundwater). תשובה C מסכמת זאת.

questions.q: 3. The word 'staggering' in the first paragraph is closest in meaning to:
options: slow
astonishing
predictable
illegal
questions.explanation: אוצר מילים: המילה staggering פירושה 'מדהים' או 'בקצב מסחרר'. המילה המקבילה היא astonishing.

questions.q: 4. Why is recycling e-waste described as a 'complex process'?
options: Because no one knows how to recycle glass.
Because it is illegal in many countries.
Because electronic components are intricate and hard to separate.
Because it requires large amounts of water.
questions.explanation: הבנת טקסט: בפסקה השנייה נכתב שהמכשירים מורכבים מחומרים שונים שקשה להפריד ביניהם (difficult to separate). לכן התהליך מורכב.

questions.q: 5. What solution do environmentalists suggest in the final sentence?
options: Banning the production of all new smartphones.
Burying e-waste in specialized underground facilities.
Shipping all e-waste to developing countries.
Creating products that are built to be easily repaired and recycled.
questions.explanation: הסקת מסקנות והבנת עמדת הכותב: שומרי הסביבה תומכים (advocate) בכלכלה מעגלית שבה היצרנים מעצבים מוצרים שקלים לתיקון ומחזור (easier to repair and recycle).

customTexts.title: The Bystander Effect
customTexts.content:
In social psychology, the bystander effect is a phenomenon in which individuals are less likely to offer help to a victim when other people are present. The greater the number of bystanders, the less likely it is that any one of them will intervene. This was widely popularized after the tragic murder of Kitty Genovese in 1964, where reportedly many witnesses saw or heard the attack but failed to call the police.

Psychologists attribute this behavior to the diffusion of responsibility. When an individual is alone, they feel the entire burden of taking action. However, in a crowd, the responsibility is shared, leading people to assume that someone else will handle the situation. Additionally, the anonymity of a crowd allows individuals to avoid the potential embarrassment of overreacting to a situation that might not actually be an emergency. This counterintuitive behavior highlights the powerful impact of social environments on individual decision-making.

questions.q: 1. What is the central idea of the text?
options: People are naturally selfish and rarely want to help others.
The presence of others decreases an individual's likelihood of helping someone in need.
Kitty Genovese was murdered because there were no witnesses around.
Calling the police during an emergency is often an overreaction.
questions.explanation: שאלת רעיון מרכזי: הטקסט כולו סובב סביב 'אפקט הצופה מהצד', שבו נוכחות של אנשים אחרים מקטינה את הסיכוי שאדם בודד יעזור.

questions.q: 2. According to the text, why do people hesitate to act when in a large group?
options: They assume someone else will take the responsibility to help.
They are physically blocked from reaching the victim.
They believe the police have already been notified.
They do not know how to perform first aid.
questions.explanation: איתור פרטים/סיבה: בפסקה השנייה מוסבר פיזור האחריות (diffusion of responsibility) – בקהל האחריות מתחלקת ואנשים מניחים שמישהו אחר כבר יטפל בזה.

questions.q: 3. The word 'intervene' in the first paragraph is closest in meaning to:
options: ignore
watch
step in
escape
questions.explanation: אוצר מילים מהקשר: המשפט מדבר על להציע עזרה (offer help). במקרה של ריבוי צופים, הסיכוי שמישהו יעזור או 'יתערב' קטן. המילה הנרדפת ל-intervene היא step in (להתערב/להיכנס לעובי הקורה).

questions.q: 4. What role did the Kitty Genovese case play in relation to the bystander effect?
options: It proved that the bystander effect is a myth.
It was the first time the police arrested a bystander.
It brought the phenomenon to public attention.
It showed that people in 1964 were more helpful than today.
questions.explanation: איתור פרטים והסקה: הטקסט אומר שהתופעה הפכה למוכרת/פופולרית (widely popularized) בעקבות המקרה של קיטי ג'נובזה. כלומר, המקרה הביא את התופעה לתשומת לב הציבור.

questions.q: 5. What is one additional reason, apart from shared responsibility, that stops people from helping?
options: Fear of the attacker.
Fear of acting foolishly if it isn't a real emergency.
Lack of communication skills.
The desire to remain famous.
questions.explanation: סריקה ממוקדת: לקראת סוף הטקסט כתוב שבאנונימיות של קהל אנשים נמנעים ממבוכה של תגובת-יתר למצב שאולי אינו חירום אמיתי (avoid the potential embarrassment of overreacting).

customTexts.title: Unlocking the Past: The Rosetta Stone
customTexts.content:
For centuries, the ancient Egyptian hieroglyphs were a complete mystery to scholars. The complex symbols adorned the walls of temples and tombs, yet their meaning was lost to time. This changed in 1799 with the discovery of the Rosetta Stone by French soldiers in Egypt. This monumental artifact proved to be the key to deciphering the ancient script.

The stone contains a royal decree issued in 196 BCE, written in three different scripts: Ancient Egyptian hieroglyphs, Demotic script, and Ancient Greek. Because scholars could still read Ancient Greek, they were able to use it as a reference point to translate the other two languages. The bilingual nature of the inscription allowed the French scholar Jean-François Champollion to finally crack the code in 1822, opening a window into the history, religion, and daily life of ancient Egypt.

questions.q: 1. What was the main obstacle for scholars before 1799?
options: They could not find any Egyptian temples or tombs.
They were unable to read or understand Egyptian hieroglyphs.
The French army prohibited them from studying history.
They did not know how to read Ancient Greek.
questions.explanation: איתור פרטים/הסקה: במשפט הראשון נאמר שההירוגליפים המצריים היו תעלומה מוחלטת עבור המלומדים במשך מאות שנים.

questions.q: 2. Why was the Rosetta Stone so crucial for translation?
options: It was the largest stone ever found in Egypt.
It contained a dictionary of all ancient languages.
It featured the same text written in a known language and an unknown language.
It was written entirely in French by the soldiers who found it.
questions.explanation: רעיון מרכזי של פסקה: הפסקה השנייה מסבירה שהאבן הכילה את אותו הטקסט ביוונית עתיקה (שפה מוכרת) ובהירוגליפים (שפה לא מוכרת), מה שאיפשר להשתמש ביוונית כנקודת ייחוס לתרגום.

questions.q: 3. The word 'deciphering' in the first paragraph is closest in meaning to:
options: destroying
creating
translating
hiding
questions.explanation: אוצר מילים מהקשר: אם האבן הייתה המפתח להבנת השפה שאבדה בזמן, deciphering משמעותו 'לפענח' או 'לתרגם' (translating).

questions.q: 4. What is the subject of the text written on the Rosetta Stone?
options: A map to hidden treasure.
A royal decree.
Instructions for building pyramids.
A Greek poem.
questions.explanation: סריקה ממוקדת: תחילת הפסקה השנייה אומרת במפורש: 'The stone contains a royal decree' (האבן מכילה צו מלכותי).

questions.q: 5. What was the ultimate result of Champollion's work?
options: He became the king of Egypt.
He proved that the Greeks built the Egyptian temples.
He created a new script for modern Egyptians.
He enabled the world to understand ancient Egyptian history and culture.
questions.explanation: הסקה ממשפט סיכום: המשפט האחרון אומר ששמפוליון פיצח את הקוד, מה שפתח חלון להיסטוריה, לדת ולחיי היומיום של מצרים העתיקה.

customTexts.title: The Plight of the Great Barrier Reef
customTexts.content:
The Great Barrier Reef, located off the coast of Australia, is the world's largest coral reef system and a site of unparalleled marine biodiversity. However, this natural wonder is currently facing an existential threat due to climate change. Rising ocean temperatures have led to widespread coral bleaching. When water is too warm, corals expel the algae living in their tissues, causing them to turn completely white and become highly susceptible to disease and death.

While corals can survive a bleaching event, severe and repeated occurrences leave them with little time to recover. Environmentalists argue that local efforts, such as reducing water pollution and restricting fishing, are helpful but insufficient. To truly mitigate the damage and save the reef, a global reduction in greenhouse gas emissions is absolutely imperative.

questions.q: 1. What is the primary cause of coral bleaching according to the text?
options: Overfishing in the Australian coast.
Abnormally high ocean temperatures.
The natural aging process of corals.
A specific disease carried by marine algae.
questions.explanation: איתור פרטים/סיבה ותוצאה: הטקסט כותב בפירוש: 'Rising ocean temperatures have led to widespread coral bleaching' (עליית טמפרטורות האוקיינוס הובילה להלבנת אלמוגים נרחבת).

questions.q: 2. What happens to corals when they undergo bleaching?
options: They immediately die and sink to the bottom.
They migrate to colder ocean regions.
They lose their algae, turn white, and become vulnerable.
They absorb more greenhouse gases from the water.
questions.explanation: איתור פרטים מדויק: בהמשך הפסקה הראשונה מוסבר שהם פולטים את האצות, הופכים ללבנים, ונעשים פגיעים (susceptible) למחלות.

questions.q: 3. The word 'mitigate' in the second paragraph is closest in meaning to:
options: worsen
measure
lessen
ignore
questions.explanation: אוצר מילים מהקשר: השורה אומרת 'כדי באמת ___ את הנזק ולהציל את השונית'. המטרה היא להפחית או למתן את הנזק, ולכן lessen היא התשובה הנכונה (mitigate = למתן/להקל).

questions.q: 4. What is the author's view on local efforts to save the reef?
options: They are completely useless and a waste of money.
They are the only way to save the coral reefs.
They are beneficial, but a global solution is needed.
They are causing more harm than climate change.
questions.explanation: הבנת עמדת הכותב: בפסקה השנייה הכותב מציין שמאמצים מקומיים (כמו צמצום זיהום) הם 'helpful but insufficient' (עוזרים אך אינם מספיקים) ונדרשת הפחתה גלובלית של פליטות.

questions.q: 5. Which of the following is NOT true regarding a bleached coral?
options: It has expelled its algae.
It has turned white.
It is permanently dead and cannot recover.
It is more prone to diseases.
questions.explanation: שאלת NOT/EXCEPT - אלימינציה: הפסקה השנייה פותחת במשפט 'While corals can survive a bleaching event...' (בעוד שאלמוגים יכולים לשרוד אירוע הלבנה). לכן, הטענה שהם 'מתים לצמיתות ולא יכולים להחלים' (C) אינה נכונה.

customTexts.title: The Enigma of Black Holes
customTexts.content:
Black holes are among the most mysterious and fascinating objects in the universe. A black hole is a region of space where the gravitational pull is so intense that nothing, not even light, can escape it. They are typically formed when a massive star collapses in on itself at the end of its life cycle. The boundary surrounding a black hole, beyond which nothing can return, is known as the event horizon.

For decades, scientists believed that black holes simply devour everything and grow infinitely. However, physicist Stephen Hawking proposed a groundbreaking theory: black holes actually emit tiny particles of radiation, now known as Hawking radiation. This implies that black holes are not completely dark and eternal. Instead, over unfathomable amounts of time, they slowly lose mass and eventually evaporate, resolving a long-standing paradox in quantum mechanics.

questions.q: 1. How are typical black holes formed according to the text?
options: By the collision of two planets.
From the collapse of a very large star.
When light gets trapped in empty space.
By the sudden expansion of the universe.
questions.explanation: איתור פרטים: הפסקה הראשונה קובעת מפורשות: 'typically formed when a massive star collapses in on itself' (נוצרים כשכוכב מסיבי קורס אל תוך עצמו).

questions.q: 2. What is the 'event horizon'?
options: The center of a black hole.
The light emitted by a collapsing star.
The point of no return surrounding a black hole.
The scientific term for Hawking radiation.
questions.explanation: הבנת הגדרות בטקסט: המשפט האחרון בפסקה הראשונה מגדיר את אופק האירועים כ-'The boundary... beyond which nothing can return' (הגבול שמעבר לו שום דבר לא יכול לחזור).

questions.q: 3. The word 'emit' in the second paragraph is closest in meaning to:
options: absorb
release
destroy
hide
questions.explanation: אוצר מילים מהקשר: התיאוריה של הוקינג טוענת שחורים שחורים פולטים (emit) קרינה, בניגוד לאמונה שהם רק שואבים/בולעים. המילה הנרדפת היא release (לשחרר/לפלוט).

questions.q: 4. What did Stephen Hawking's theory suggest about the lifespan of a black hole?
options: They live forever and never change size.
They will eventually expand and consume the universe.
They slowly lose mass and will ultimately evaporate.
They turn back into stars after billions of years.
questions.explanation: איתור מסקנות/הסקה: הטקסט מסביר שבזכות הקרינה שהם פולטים, החורים השחורים לאט לאט מאבדים מסה ובסופו של דבר מתאדים (slowly lose mass and eventually evaporate).

questions.q: 5. Why was Hawking's theory considered 'groundbreaking'?
options: Because it proved that black holes do not exist.
Because it contradicted the long-held belief that nothing can ever escape a black hole.
Because it allowed humans to travel through event horizons.
Because it was the first time someone used a telescope to see a black hole.
questions.explanation: הבנת משמעות והקשר: התיאוריה הייתה פורצת דרך כי במשך עשורים מדענים האמינו ששום דבר לא יוצא משם (devour everything). הוקינג הוכיח שקרינה כן נפלטת החוצה, מה שסתר את האמונה הרווחת.

customTexts.title: The Impacts of the Industrial Revolution
customTexts.content:
The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the late 18th century, marked a massive transition from hand production methods to machines, new chemical manufacturing, and iron production processes. This era sparked an unprecedented economic boom and fundamentally altered the daily lives of millions. Driven by the invention of the steam engine, factories sprouted across the country, demanding a huge workforce.

As a result, a massive wave of urbanization occurred. People left agricultural rural areas to seek employment in the cities. While this led to the mass production of affordable goods, it also brought severe social challenges. The rapid influx of people resulted in overcrowded cities, poor sanitation, and squalid living conditions for the working class. Moreover, labor laws were virtually non-existent, leading to the exploitation of workers, including young children.

questions.q: 1. What was the primary technological driver of the factory boom?
options: The invention of electricity.
The discovery of chemical manufacturing.
The creation of the steam engine.
The development of agricultural tools.
questions.explanation: איתור פרטים ממוקד: בסוף הפסקה הראשונה נאמר: 'Driven by the invention of the steam engine, factories sprouted...' (מונע על ידי המצאת מנוע הקיטור, צצו מפעלים...).

questions.q: 2. What does 'urbanization' refer to in the context of the text?
options: The transition from using hand tools to machines.
The migration of people from rural farms to cities.
The process of purifying city water supplies.
The exploitation of children in factories.
questions.explanation: הבנת מושג מהקשר (Context): מיד אחרי המילה urbanization מופיע משפט שמסביר אותה: 'People left agricultural rural areas to seek employment in the cities' (אנשים עזבו אזורים כפריים לטובת ערים).

questions.q: 3. The word 'squalid' in the second paragraph is closest in meaning to:
options: luxurious
spacious
filthy
organized
questions.explanation: אוצר מילים מהקשר: המילה 'squalid' מתארת את תנאי המחיה, המופיעים ברשימה של דברים שליליים עקב צפיפות: 'overcrowded cities, poor sanitation...' (ערים צפופות, תברואה גרועה). התשובה המתאימה היא filthy (מלוכלך/מטונף).

questions.q: 4. Which of the following was a POSITIVE outcome of the Industrial Revolution mentioned in the text?
options: The establishment of strict labor laws.
The mass production of affordable goods.
The improvement of urban sanitation.
The decrease in city populations.
questions.explanation: סריקה ואלימינציה: הפסקה השנייה מונה חסרונות רבים, אך מציינת יתרון אחד: 'While this led to the mass production of affordable goods...' (ייצור המוני של מוצרים ברי-השגה).

questions.q: 5. What is the author's overall perspective on the Industrial Revolution?
options: It was a period of purely technological triumph with no drawbacks.
It was a catastrophic event that destroyed the British economy.
It brought significant economic and technological progress, but at a severe human cost.
It had very little real impact on the daily lives of ordinary people.
questions.explanation: שאלת גישת הכותב (Main Idea/Tone): הכותב מציג תמונה מורכבת (Balanced). מצד אחד שגשוג כלכלי (economic boom) וייצור המוני, ומצד שני צפיפות, הזנחה וניצול עובדים (severe human cost).

customAssocs.quality: קואלה זו חיה איכותית לא סתם חיה
customAssocs.vulnerable: בלי וילון אתה פגיע כולם רואים אותך
customAssocs.accusation: ac use- יש לי אשמה על שימוש לרעה
customAssocs.herd: איר-לשמוע. שאתה אוסף את העדר הם שומעים בקולך
customAssocs.regret: ר-גרט-לחזור מנהדר-היה לא טוב אז מתלונן
customAssocs.impartial: part-אי פארט ללא חלק פנוי ולא נוטה לשום חלק
